Survived.one_line_result: Net Sharpe 0.57, max drawdown -15%, 5.7% CAGR net of 5 bps.
Survived.primary_evidence: Net Sharpe 0.57 at 5 bps with -15.3% max drawdown (SPY drew down -55.2%).
Survived.main_weakness: Performance is uneven across market regimes.
Survived.best_use_case: Use as a long-only de-risking trend overlay that controls drawdowns, not as a standalone return-maximizing strategy.
Survived.final_research_note: Time-Series Momentum holds up: net Sharpe 0.57, -15.3% max drawdown, and out-of-sample Sharpe 0.48. It survives realistic costs and parameter variation.

Conditional.one_line_result: Net Sharpe 0.58, max drawdown -12%, 4.3% CAGR net of 5 bps.
Conditional.primary_evidence: Net Sharpe 0.58 at 5 bps with -11.8% max drawdown (SPY drew down -55.2%).
Conditional.main_weakness: Performance is uneven across market regimes.
Conditional.best_use_case: Use as a volatility-control overlay when drawdown control matters more than raw return, not as a return maximizer.
Conditional.final_research_note: Volatility-Scaled Momentum is conditional: it adds value mainly through risk control (net Sharpe 0.58, -11.8% max drawdown) rather than raw return, and depends on costs or regime.

Rejected.one_line_result: Net Sharpe 0.45, max drawdown -29%, 5.2% CAGR net of 5 bps.
